project.name: veeam-healthcheck
project.description: Veeam Health Check tool — generates configuration and sizing reports for VBR installations

watches.claim: VBR v12+: VMware proxy supports 2 concurrent tasks per core and 1 task per 1 GB RAM
watches.description: Proxy task capacity formula for v12+ — doubling the v11 ratio. Used in CProxyDataFormer.cs to calculate max concurrency.

watches.claim: VBR v11: VMware proxy supports 1 concurrent task per core and 1 task per 2 GB RAM
watches.description: Proxy task capacity formula for v11 — lower ratio than v12+. Used in CProxyDataFormer.cs version branch.

watches.claim: VBR repository and gateway servers support 3 concurrent tasks per CPU core and require 4 GB RAM per task
watches.description: Repository/gateway concurrency formula in CDataTypesParser.cs — coreTasks = cores × 3, memTasks = RAM ÷ 4.

watches.claim: VBR NAS/file proxy (GP proxy) requires 2 CPU cores and 4 GB RAM per concurrent task
watches.description: GP proxy is far more resource-intensive than VP proxy. Encoded in VbrConfig.json as 2 cores/task and 4 GB/task.

watches.claim: VBR CDP proxy requires a fixed minimum of 4 CPU cores and 8 GB RAM regardless of task count
watches.description: CDP proxy sizing is not per-task; it has a fixed floor. Encoded in VbrConfig.json as CDP_cores=4, CDP_ram=8.

watches.claim: VBR Backup Server requires minimum 4 CPU cores and 8 GB RAM for OS overhead on v12, and 8 cores / 16 GB RAM on v13+
watches.description: OS overhead floor for the backup server itself, before any task math. Version-branched in Invoke-VhcConcurrencyAnalysis.ps1.

watches.claim: VBR v13 Security Compliance Analyzer results are accessible via Get-VBRSecurityComplianceAnalyzerResults and expose Id, Note, Status, and Type fields
watches.description: SCA rule collection depends on this cmdlet signature. The health check maps rule IDs to human-readable guidance from VbrConfig.json.

watches.claim: VBR NAS backup job sessions write unstructured data statistics under '=====UNSTRUCTURED DATA====' and '=====NAS INFRASTRUCTURE====' sections in the VMC.log file
watches.description: NAS log parsing in Get-NasInfo.ps1 depends on these exact section headers in VMC.log to extract share stats.

watches.claim: VBR Gateway server minimum OS floor is 2 CPU cores and 4 GB RAM
watches.description: Gateway server OS overhead minimum used in multi-role server calculations. Corrected from earlier 1-core assumption per ADR 0010.

watches.claim: VBR stores its active database configuration (PostgreSQL vs MSSQL, connection details) in the Windows registry at HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Veeam\Veeam Backup and Replication
watches.description: Registry path used by Get-SqlSName.ps1 to discover the active database backend. SqlActiveConfiguration key determines PostgreSQL vs MSSQL branch.
